name: AWARE
tagline: Hearing & brain fitness. Works alongside hearing aids, AirPods, or nothing at all.

voice.tone: Audiologist who is also a friend. Clinical authority + plain language. Second person. Celebrate consistency over scores. Acknowledge emotional weight without drama.
do: Use plain language ('trouble hearing in noise', not 'auditory processing deficit')
Position AWARE as bolt-on to ANY hearing aid
do_not: Claim AWARE prevents/treats/slows dementia or cognitive decline
Use medical-diagnostic language without 'may help' framing

style_anti_references: Stock photo silver-haired couple in wheat field
Generic hearing aid manufacturer marketing imagery
Anything that looks like a senior living home brochure
illustration_style: Flat geometric illustration, teal #0d9488 dominant, generous whitespace, soft cream background, 2-3 color limit, no photorealism. Audiogram-curve motif when natural. Style of Magoz meets Headspace's Christoph Niemann meets Apple Health.
icon_style: Rounded line icons, 2px stroke, brand teal #0d9488 fill, slightly playful, consistent corner radius 4px, viewBox 24x24, SVG output. Match Phosphor Icons aesthetic with audiogram-curve influences.
audience: Adults 50-75 with mild-to-moderate hearing loss. Often resistant to 'hearing aid' identity. Tech comfort ranges from low (Doris persona) to high (Robert Sterling). Default text scale supports 1.5x.

brand_mark.description: Audiogram curve descending through 6 frequency points (500Hz / 1k / 2k / 4k / 6k / 8k). White curve + 6 white dots on teal background. NEVER add ear or person silhouette.
